sourceMovie.title: Can't Buy Me Love
sourceMovie.whyPeopleLoveIt: People revisit Can't Buy Me Love for the same reason it broke through in 1987: clear stakes, memorable faces, and a romance rhythm that doesn't stall in the middle. Steve Rash keeps the ensemble pointed in one direction. The comedy and romance blend is the engine: it lets the film shift registers without feeling like two different movies stitched together.

recommendations.title: The Kissing Booth 2
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If Can't Buy Me Love clicked for you, The Kissing Booth 2 (2020) is a strong next stop: both trade in comedy and romance, with comic timing and sharper banter. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

faq.question: What should I watch after Can't Buy Me Love?
faq.answer: Try The Kissing Booth 2, The DUFF, No Hard Feelings—each shares DNA with Can't Buy Me Love while changing the setting enough to feel fresh.

faq.question: What genre is Can't Buy Me Love?
faq.answer: Can't Buy Me Love (1987) is best described as Comedy, Romance. Stores and algorithms use different labels, but that mix is what fans actually recognize on a rewatch.

faq.question: Is Can't Buy Me Love worth revisiting?
faq.answer: Viewer scores sit around 6.9/10 on TMDB—high enough that replays are common for people who like this comedy lane.
